free_text: Excitation functions for 12-C-induced reactions on
The target assembly consisted of two thin niobium foils
(0.005 mm thickness) interspaced by aluminium
degrader-catcher foils.
The energy loss of the 12-C-particles in the degrader
and target foils was calculated using the range and
stopping power tables of Northcliffe and Schilling.
The activities produced were counted using two Ge(Li)
detectors (active volume: 55 cm**3, resolution-2.1 keV
for the 1.332 MeV 60-Co line calibrated absolutely by
means of a set of Amersham standard sources.
15 minutes and 1 hour long irradiations.
a Faraday cup behind the target assembly.
The counting periods varied between 3 minutes
and 1 day depending on the activity and the half-life
of the nuclides produced.
the beam intensity was of the order of 1 uA.
The statistical model code ALICE.
The errors given in figures include the
uncertainties of the counting rate, the target
thickness, the beam current measurement and the
efficiency of the detectors. Errors due to the nuclear
data are not taken into account.
figs 1-3 from Radiochimica Acta,28(1981)1
